mnemonics.title: Linear Transformation Definition
mnemonics.content: Think 'ADD and MULT': Additivity + Homogeneity (scalar MULTiplication) must both hold for linearity.

mnemonics.title: Rotation Matrix
mnemonics.content: COS-SIN pattern: First row [cos, -sin], second row [sin, cos]. Remember: 'Cosine goes cosine, Sine goes sine'.

mnemonics.title: Transformation Matrix Building
mnemonics.content: T(e₁) as column 1, T(e₂) as column 2 - 'Basis Images Build Inside' the matrix columns.

acronyms.expansion: Linear Transformation - preserves addition and scalar multiplication
acronyms.usage: Checking if a transformation is linear: verify T(u+v)=T(u)+T(v) and T(ku)=kT(u)

acronyms.expansion: Linear + Origin Preserving - transformations that map origin to origin
acronyms.usage: All linear transformations map zero vector to zero vector: T(0)=0

visualTips.visualization: Think of a clock hands - rotation matrix turns the entire grid like rotating a piece of paper.
Pulling or pushing the grid along axes - stretch in x-direction, squash in y-direction.
Sliding the top of a deck of cards horizontally while keeping bottom fixed.
Fold the plane along the mirror line - points flip to the other side at equal distance.
Draw a unit square and see how the transformation squashes/stretches it - area becomes |det| times original.

analogies.analogy: Like a function machine: input vector goes in, transformed vector comes out, following fixed rules.
Matrix is like a machine's instruction manual telling how to transform every point in the plane.
Putting two camera filters one after another - the combined effect is matrix multiplication.
Undo button - applying transformation then its inverse returns you to the original.
Zoom factor for area - like adjusting zoom on a map, everything scales by the determinant.
